meaning: adj.宿命的,重大的,决定性的,致命性的,预言性的
examples: She looked back now to that fateful day in December.
It was a fateful decision, one which was to break the Government.
He gave a detailed account of what happened on the fateful night.
In 2009 Stern made the fateful decision to push First Republic and its parent firm into bankruptcy court.
Some posts even mention flights they took over ten years ago and a fateful missed connection.
